actionLibraryList.title: Tabletop Thoracic Rotation
actionLibraryList.context: During the rotation, avoid rushing the movement; maintain slow, controlled motion to prevent overstretching or misalignment.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749523622522.jpg","context":"1.Raise one arm with the elbow bent and place your hand behind your head."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749523625921.jpg","context":"1.During the rotation, keep your hand gently touching or lightly supporting your head.\n2.Keep your pelvis and hips stable without moving.\n3.Position your knees hip-width apart."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale during the rotation to help relax your body.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: At the maximum range, hold for one to two seconds, feel the stretch in your thoracic spine, and keep your chest open.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: During thoracic spine rotation, keep your hips from rotating.

actionLibraryList.title: Barbell Bulgarian Split Squat
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand about two feet away from the bench.
2.Rest the barbell across your upper traps behind your neck.
3.Lift one foot and place it on the edge of the bench behind you.
4.Bend your front knee and lower down until your front thigh is parallel to the ground and your back knee is close to the floor.
5.Drive through your front heel to return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Lock the pulley block to the footboard end with the barbell attached.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Rest one leg on the bench behind you and bend your standing leg.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Keep breathing. Inhale during the downward phase, and exhale as you press to standing.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Press back to standing by pushing through your quads and glutes to power the upward phase.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"context":"1.Place the barbell across your upper traps behind your neck.
2.Keep your back straight and core engaged.
3.Bend your knees and place one foot behind you on the edge of a bench.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750838840540.jpg"},{"context":"1.Keep your back straight and core stable.
2.As you lower down, keep your knees aligned with your toes.
3.Don't let your back knee touch the ground, maintain constant muscle tension.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750838843690.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale on the way up, inhale on the way down.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage your glutes and legs for power.

actionLibraryList.title: Barbell Bench Press
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Lie on a bench, holding the barbell above the chest.
2.From the starting position, slowly lower the barbell to the mid-chest.
3.Drive through the chest and triceps to press the barbell back up to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Lock the pulleys to the footboard with the barbell attached.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Lie on the bench and grip the barbell slightly wider than shoulder-width.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Pack your shoulders, open your chest, and draw your abdomen in.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Slightly arch your lower back and don't lift your glutes off the bench.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Keep your breath steady. Exhale on your way up, and inhale on your way down.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Engage your pecs to press the barbell directly above your chest and hold the contraction for one to two seconds at the top.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Keep your forearms vertical to the ground.\n2.Slightly arch your lower back without lifting your glutes off the bench.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748921699325.jpg"},{"context":"1.Extend your arms fully without locking the elbows.\n2.Keep your shoulder blades retracted and in contact with the bench throughout the movement.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748921703510.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Inhale as you lower, exhale as you press up.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Drive through the chest to press the barbell directly overhead, pausing 1–2 seconds at the top for peak contraction.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Slightly arch your lower back, ensuring your glutes stay on the bench.

actionLibraryList.title: Barbell Deadlift
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with feet hip-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
2.Grip the barbell shoulder-width apart, palms facing forward.
3.Hinge at the hips and bend your knees to lower down, keeping your back straight and head aligned with your spine.
4.Engage your glutes and hamstrings to extend hips and knees, pulling the barbell up along your thighs.
5.Stand fully upright, tightening your glutes at the top.
6.Unlike straight-leg or Romanian deadlifts, this version focuses more on glute contraction.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Lock the pulleys to the footboard end with barbell attachment.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Stand with a neutral spine and grab the barbell with two hands.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Don't hunch your back or hyperextend your lumbar at the top of the motion.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Keep your breath steady. Inhale on your way down, exhale on your way up.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Engage your glutes and briefly hold the contraction at the top of the motion.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"context":"1.Keep your head aligned and eyes looking forward.
2.Maintain a straight back with chest up and core engaged.
3.Keep your knees aligned with your toes at all times.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750233568152.jpg"},{"context":"1.Avoid shrugging your shoulders.
2.Tighten your glutes and avoid overextending your hips.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750233571939.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale on the way up, inhale on the way down.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Drive up by engaging your glutes and hamstrings, bringing your hips and knees back in line simultaneously.

actionLibraryList.title: Plank with Hip Drop
actionLibraryList.context: Assume a standard forearm plank position with shoulders stacked directly above the elbows, legs extended, and toes on the ground. Engage your core and keep your body stable. Slowly rotate your hips from side to side, gently tapping the floor with one hip before returning to center, then alternate. Avoid sagging your lower back, shrugging your shoulders, or using momentum. Maintain stable shoulders and core activation throughout. Exhale as you rotate your hips, inhale as you return to center. Move with a smooth, controlled rhythm, focusing on engaging your obliques and core’s anti-rotational strength.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749718582256.jpg","context":"1.Keep your shoulders directly above your elbows."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749718591266.jpg","context":"1. With control, rotate your hips side to side, gently tapping the floor on each side."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you rotate your hips, inhale as you return to center.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Activation of the abdominal obliques and core anti-rotation muscles.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid sagging hips, shrugged shoulders, or using momentum to swing.

actionLibraryList.title: Pallof Press
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Set the machine attachment mount at chest height and attach the handle to the mount.
2.Stand facing sideways with the handle grip in both hands against your chest.
3.With load on the cable, press the handle straight out in front of you slowly.
4.Hold for a second, then slowly pull the handle back to your chest.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Attach a handle at around chest height.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Stand in a neutral position with two hands grasping the handle in front.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Arms are supposed to extend out fully.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Keep breathing, exhale once you press the handle forward, inhale and slowly back to the chest.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Initiate the press motion by engaging your core.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Drop your shoulders.\n2.Hold the handles with both hands.\n3.Lengthen your spine, keeping your back upright.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750745945888.jpg"},{"context":"1.Extend your hands forward and brace your core.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750745969978.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Keep breathing. Exhale as you press the handle forward, inhale as you slowly pull it back to your chest.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Initiate the press motion by engaging your core.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Arms are supposed to extend out fully.

actionLibraryList.title: Standing Cable Hip Abduction（Main Frame）
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Adjust the grips to the lowest position of the armrest.
2.Stand tall with feet hip width apart, Toes pointed fowards.
3.Slowly kick the leg with strap backwards.
4.Do not allow hips to twist, but focus on squeezing the glute.
5.Best to perfom at a slow tempo with a pause at full contracted position.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749193636942.jpg","context":"1.Keep your torso stable, put one hand on your waist."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749193641787.jpg","context":"1.Square your hips to stabilize balance.\n2.Squeeze your glutes and leverage glutes external abduction to raise your leg out to the side."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you extend outside and Inhale as you lower your leg to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Squeeze your glutes at the top.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your left foot planted, brace your core to maintain body balance.
